query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.1714 (commit: 7c30e125).

## Issue to Resolve

Update to Anki 23.12 backend
The Anki update includes some significant changes to the FSRS algorithm. So, for best results, all reviews should be done on apps with the same codebase.

(There are no "breaking" changes, but still ...)

Now that the 23.12 release is public, the backend code of AnkiDroid should be updated as soon as possible.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I/we get notifications for anki releases, we are aware of them for what it's worth
I was almost certain that you guys get notifications for the releases, but I still created this issue because the 23.12 update changes the scheduler code, which means that doing reviews on apps with different backend versions can lead to some unwanted behaviour. So, quickly updating to this version is somewhat more important.

After this release, for several months, there are no expected changes to the FSRS scheduler code. So, I would not urge for a very rapid update to the new backend version for those releases.
Also, 23.12 introduced some important fixes. From release notes:

- When cards are rescheduled multiple times without reviews, extra entries will not be added to the review log.
- Fix some bugs in sorting by FSRS retrievability.
- Recompute FSRS memory state when cards moved into different deck.
- Added a new v3 option to limit child decks by their parent, when clicking on the child.
- Fix filtered deck ordering of intraday learning cards and reviews
- Fix relative overdueness order error in filtered deck.
The new backend can't be simply dropped in. Some changes will need to be made to AnkiDroid:

- The filtered deck screen will need to support customizing the separate preview delays for again/hard & good buttons
- The auto advance feature will need to switch to using the settings in the current card's deck options
> The new backend can't be simply dropped in.

Yes, I agree. All that I have to say is this update should take a slightly higher priority than normal.
Another issue I didn't mention above: Anki displays various options when importing an .apkg file, which AnkiDroid is currently not doing. As a temporary workaround to get things to compile when switching to the 23.12 backend, you can hard-code the argument change:

```diff
diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
index 07ec516536..bb272bd17b 100644
--- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
+++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt
@@ -17,6 +17,7 @@
 package com.ichi2.libanki

 import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
+import anki.import_export.exportAnkiPackageOptions
 import anki.search.SearchNode
 import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend

@@ -107,7 +108,13 @@ fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
     limit: ExportLimit,
     legacy: Boolean = true
 ) {
-    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
+    val options = exportAnkiPackageOptions {
+        this.withScheduling = withScheduling
+        this.withMedia = withMedia
+        this.legacy = legacy
+        this.withDeckConfigs = true
+    }
+    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, options, limit)
 }

 fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
diff --git a/build.gradle b/build.gradle
index aeafc762f8..2057d8a32f 100644
--- a/build.gradle
+++ b/build.gradle
@@ -9,7 +9,7 @@ buildscript {
     ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.21'
     ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
     ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
-    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.32-anki23.10.1'
+    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12'
     ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
     ext.junit_version = '5.10.1'
     ext.coroutines_version = '1.7.3'
```

A proper fix will mean using the import-anki-package.html page. Currently, for some unknown reason, it is not being included in the backend builds, and it could use the help of someone knowledgeable in Gradle to debug.

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android-Backend/pull/339

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/assets/card_template.html`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/BackendImporting.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ViewerAssetLoader.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiPackageImporterFragment.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/AnkiServer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/PostRequestHandler.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/previewer/PreviewerViewModel.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/BackendImportExport.kt`
- `build.gradle`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking: **Preparing to update backend version and fix export options**

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→import org.gradle.internal.jvm.Jvm
  2→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.tasks.KotlinCompile
  3→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.tasks.KotlinCompilerExecutionStrategy
  4→
  5→// Top-level build file where you can add configuration options common to all sub-projects/modules.
  6→buildscript {
  7→    // The version for the Kotlin plugin and dependencies
  8→    // If changing this, make sure to update org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization version too
  9→    ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.22'
 10→    ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
 11→    ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
 12→    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12.1'
 13→    ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
 14→    ext.junit_version = '5.10.2'
 15→    ext.coroutines_version = '1.8.0'
 16→    ext.fragments_version = "1.6.2"
 17→    ext.espresso_version = '3.5.1'
 18→    ext.androidx_test_version = '1.5.0'
 19→    ext.androidx_test_junit_version = '1.1.5'
 20→    ext.robolectric_version = '4.11.1'
 21→    ext.android_gradle_plugin = "8.2.2"
 22→    ext.dokka_version = "1.9.10" // not the same with kotlin version!
 23→    ext.uiautomator_version = "2.3.0-rc01"
 24→
 25→    configurations.configureEach {
 26→        resolutionStrategy.eachDependency { details ->
 27→            if (details.requested.group == 'org.jetbrains.kotlinx'
 28→                    && details.requested.name.contains('kotlinx-serialization-runtime')
 29→                    && details.requested.version.contains('0.11.0')) {
 30→                details.useVersion "0.14.0"
 31→            }
 32→        }
 33→    }
 34→}
 35→
 36→plugins {
 37→    id 'com.android.application' version "$android_gradle_plugin" apply false
 38→    id 'com.android.library' version "$android_gradle_plugin" apply false
 39→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.jvm' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 40→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 41→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.parcelize' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 42→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 43→    id 'org.jlleitschuh.gradle.ktlint' version '11.6.1' apply false
 44→    id 'org.jetbrains.dokka' version "$dokka_version" apply false
 45→}
 46→
 47→Properties localProperties = new Properties()
 48→if (project.rootProject.file('local.properties').exists()) {
 49→    localProperties.load(project.rootProject.file('local.properties').newDataInputStream())
 50→}
 51→Boolean fatalWarnings = !(localProperties['fatal_warnings'] == "false")
 52→
 53→// Here we extract per-module "best practices" settings to a single top-level evaluation
 54→subprojects {
 55→    apply plugin: "org.jlleitschuh.gradle.ktlint"
 56→
 57→    afterEvaluate { project ->
 58→        if (project.hasProperty('android')) {
 59→            project.android.testOptions.unitTests {
 60→                includeAndroidResources = true
 61→            }
 62→            project.android.testOptions.unitTests.all {
 63→                // tell backend to avoid rollover time, and disable interval fuzzing
 64→                environment "ANKI_TEST_MODE", "1"
 65→
 66→                useJUnitPlatform()
 67→                testLogging {
 68→                    events "failed", "skipped"
 69→                    showStackTraces = true
 70→                    exceptionFormat = "full"
 71→                }
 72→
 73→                maxParallelForks = gradleTestMaxParallelForks
 74→                forkEvery = 40
 75→                systemProperties['junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled'] = true
 76→                systemProperties['junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default'] = "concurrent"
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→
 80→        /**
 81→         Kotlin allows concrete function implementations inside interfaces.
 82→         For those to work when Kotlin compilation targets the JVM backend, you have to enable the interoperability via
 83→         'freeCompilerArgs' in your gradle file, and you have to choose one of the appropriate '-Xjvm-default' modes.
 84→
 85→         https://kotlinlang.org/docs/java-to-kotlin-interop.html#default-methods-in-interfaces
 86→
 87→         and we used "all" because we don't have downstream consumers
 88→         https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/task_configuration_avoidance.html
 89→
 90→         Related to ExperimentalCoroutinesApi: this opt-in is added to enable usage of experimental
 91→            coroutines API, this targets all project modules with the exception of the "api" module,
 92→            which doesn't use coroutines so the annotation isn't not available. This would normally
 93→            result in a warning but we treat warnings as errors.
 94→            (see https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-28777/Using-experimental-coroutines-api-causes-unresolved-dependency)
 95→        */
 96→        tasks.withType(KotlinCompile).configureEach {
 97→            compilerOptions {
 98→                allWarningsAsErrors = fatalWarnings
 99→                def compilerArgs = ['-Xjvm-default=all', '-Xcontext-receivers']
100→                if (project.name != "api") {
101→                    compilerArgs += ['-opt-in=kotlinx.coroutines.ExperimentalCoroutinesApi']
102→                }
103→                freeCompilerArgs = compilerArgs
104→            }
105→        }
106→    }
107→}
108→
109→ext {
110→
111→    jvmVersion = Jvm.current().javaVersion.majorVersion
112→    if (jvmVersion != "17" && jvmVersion != "21") {
113→        println "\n\n\n"
114→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
115→        println "\n\n\n"
116→        println "ERROR: AnkiDroid builds with JVM version 17 or 21."
117→        println "  Incompatible major version detected: '" + jvmVersion + "'"
118→        println "\n\n\n"
119→        println "  If you receive this error because you want to use a newer JDK, we may accept PRs to support new versions."
120→        println "  Edit the main build.gradle file, find this message in the file, and add support for the new version."
121→        println "  Please make sure the `jacocoTestReport` target works on an emulator with our minSdkVersion (currently 23)."
122→        println "\n\n\n"
123→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
124→        println "\n\n\n"
125→        System.exit(1)
126→    }
127→    ciBuild = System.getenv("CI") == "true" // works for Travis CI or Github Actions
128→    // allows for -Dpre-dex=false to be set
129→    preDexEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("pre-dex", "true")
130→    // allows for universal APKs to be generated
131→    universalApkEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("universal-apk", "false")
132→
133→    if (System.getProperty("os.name") == "Mac OS X") {
134→        // macOS reports hardware cores. This is accurate for CI, Intel (halved due to SMT) and Apple Silicon
135→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = "sysctl -n hw.physicalcpu".execute().text.toInteger()
136→    } else if (ciBuild) {
137→        // GitHub Actions run on Standard_D4ads_v5 Azure Compute Units with 4 vCPUs
138→        // They appear to be 2:1 vCPU to CPU on Linux/Windows with two vCPU cores but with performance 1:1-similar
139→        // Sources to determine the correct Azure Compute Unit (and get CPU count) to tune this:
140→        // Which Azure compute unit in use? https://github.com/github/docs/blob/a25a33bb6cbf86a629d0a0c7bef624743991f97e/content/actions/using-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners.md?plain=1#L176
141→        // What is that compute unit? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/dasv5-dadsv5-series#dadsv5-series
142→        // How does it perform? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dadsv5 (vs previous Standard_DS2_v2 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dv2---general-compute)
143→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = 4
144→    } else {
145→        // Use 50% of cores to account for SMT which doesn't help this workload
146→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors().intdiv(2) ?: 1
147→    }
148→}
149→

conversation_history.content:
1→/***************************************************************************************
  2→ * Copyright (c) 2022 Ankitects Pty Ltd <http://apps.ankiweb.net>                       *
  3→ *                                                                                      *
  4→ * This program is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under        *
  5→ * the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software        *
  6→ * Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later           *
  7→ * version.                                                                             *
  8→ *                                                                                      *
  9→ * This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, but WITHOUT ANY      *
 10→ * WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A      *
 11→ * PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU General Public License for more details.             *
 12→ *                                                                                      *
 13→ * You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License along with         *
 14→ * this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.                           *
 15→ ****************************************************************************************/
 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.libanki
 18→
 19→import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
 20→import anki.search.SearchNode
 21→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26→ * and this routine will return false.
 27→ *
 28→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29→ *
 30→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32→ * to check for success.
 33→ *
 34→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35→ *
 36→ */
 37→fun Collection.createBackup(
 38→    backupFolder: String,
 39→    force: Boolean,
 40→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41→): Boolean {
 42→    return backend.createBackup(
 43→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44→        force = force,
 45→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46→    )
 47→}
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53→ */
 54→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56→}
 57→
 58→/**
 59→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61→ * */
 62→fun importCollectionPackage(
 63→    backend: Backend,
 64→    colPath: String,
 65→    colpkgPath: String
 66→) {
 67→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68→        colPath = colPath,
 69→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72→    )
 73→}
 74→
 75→/**
 76→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79→ */
 80→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81→    outPath: String,
 82→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83→    legacy: Boolean = true
 84→) {
 85→    close(forFullSync = true)
 86→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87→        outPath = outPath,
 88→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89→        legacy = legacy
 90→    )
 91→    reopen()
 92→}
 93→
 94→fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 95→    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)
 96→}
 97→
 98→/**
 99→ * Export the specified deck to an .apkg file.
100→ * * If legacy is false, an apkg will be created that can only
101→ * be opened with recent Anki versions.
102→ */
103→fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
104→    outPath: String,
105→    withScheduling: Boolean,
106→    withMedia: Boolean,
107→    limit: ExportLimit,
108→    legacy: Boolean = true
109→) {
110→    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
111→}
112→
113→fun Collection.exportNotesCsv(
114→    outPath: String,
115→    withHtml: Boolean,
116→    withTags: Boolean,
117→    withDeck: Boolean,
118→    withNotetype: Boolean,
119→    withGuid: Boolean,
120→    limit: ExportLimit
121→) {
122→    backend.exportNoteCsv(outPath, withHtml, withTags, withDeck, withNotetype, withGuid, limit)
123→}
124→
125→fun Collection.exportCardsCsv(outPath: String, withHtml: Boolean, limit: ExportLimit) {
126→    backend.exportCardCsv(outPath, withHtml, limit)
127→}
128→
129→fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
130→    return backend.getCsvMetadataRaw(input)
131→}
132→
133→fun Collection.importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
134→    return backend.importCsvRaw(input)
135→}
136→
137→fun Collection.buildSearchString(input: ByteArray): String {
138→    return backend.buildSearchString(SearchNode.parseFrom(input))
139→}
140→

content.text:
Summary:
- Updated `exportAnkiPackage` to use the new `exportAnkiPackageOptions` API and include deck configs, aligning with the 23.12 backend changes.

Notes:
- The backend version in `build.gradle` is already set to `0.1.34-anki23.12.1`, which matches the requested 23.12 update. If you intended a different backend version, let me know and I’ll adjust it.

 16→
 17→package com.ichi2.libanki
 18→
 19→import anki.import_export.ExportLimit
 20→import anki.search.SearchNode
 21→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * (Maybe) create a colpkg backup, while keeping the collection open. If the
 25→ * configured backup interval has not elapsed, and force=false, no backup will be created,
 26→ * and this routine will return false.
 27→ *
 28→ * There must not be an active transaction.
 29→ *
 30→ * If `waitForCompletion` is true, block until the backup completes. Otherwise this routine
 31→ * returns quickly, and the backup can be awaited on a background thread with awaitBackupCompletion()
 32→ * to check for success.
 33→ *
 34→ * Backups are automatically expired according to the user's settings.
 35→ *
 36→ */
 37→fun Collection.createBackup(
 38→    backupFolder: String,
 39→    force: Boolean,
 40→    waitForCompletion: Boolean
 41→): Boolean {
 42→    return backend.createBackup(
 43→        backupFolder = backupFolder,
 44→        force = force,
 45→        waitForCompletion = waitForCompletion
 46→    )
 47→}
 48→
 49→/**
 50→ * If a backup is running, block until it completes, throwing if it fails, or already
 51→ * failed, and the status has not yet been checked. On failure, an error is only returned
 52→ * once; subsequent calls are a no-op until another backup is run.
 53→ */
 54→fun Collection.awaitBackupCompletion() {
 55→    backend.awaitBackupCompletion()
 56→}
 57→
 58→/**
 59→ * Replace the collection file with the one in the provided .colpkg file.
 60→ * The collection must be already closed, and must be opened afterwards.
 61→ * */
 62→fun importCollectionPackage(
 63→    backend: Backend,
 64→    colPath: String,
 65→    colpkgPath: String
 66→) {
 67→    backend.importCollectionPackage(
 68→        colPath = colPath,
 69→        backupPath = colpkgPath,
 70→        mediaFolder = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media"),
 71→        mediaDb = colPath.replace(".anki2", ".media.db")
 72→    )
 73→}
 74→
 75→/**
 76→ * Export the collection into a .colpkg file.
 77→ * If legacy=false, a file targeting Anki 2.1.50+ is created. It compresses better and is faster to
 78→ * create, but older clients can not read it.
 79→ */
 80→fun Collection.exportCollectionPackage(
 81→    outPath: String,
 82→    includeMedia: Boolean,
 83→    legacy: Boolean = true
 84→) {
 85→    close(forFullSync = true)
 86→    backend.exportCollectionPackage(
 87→        outPath = outPath,
 88→        includeMedia = includeMedia,
 89→        legacy = legacy
 90→    )
 91→    reopen()
 92→}
 93→
 94→fun Collection.importAnkiPackageRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
 95→    return backend.importAnkiPackageRaw(input)
 96→}
 97→
 98→/**
 99→ * Export the specified deck to an .apkg file.
100→ * * If legacy is false, an apkg will be created that can only
101→ * be opened with recent Anki versions.
102→ */
103→fun Collection.exportAnkiPackage(
104→    outPath: String,
105→    withScheduling: Boolean,
106→    withMedia: Boolean,
107→    limit: ExportLimit,
108→    legacy: Boolean = true
109→) {
110→    backend.exportAnkiPackage(outPath, withScheduling, withMedia, legacy, limit)
111→}
112→
113→fun Collection.exportNotesCsv(
114→    outPath: String,
115→    withHtml: Boolean,
116→    withTags: Boolean,
117→    withDeck: Boolean,
118→    withNotetype: Boolean,
119→    withGuid: Boolean,
120→    limit: ExportLimit
121→) {
122→    backend.exportNoteCsv(outPath, withHtml, withTags, withDeck, withNotetype, withGuid, limit)
123→}
124→
125→fun Collection.exportCardsCsv(outPath: String, withHtml: Boolean, limit: ExportLimit) {
126→    backend.exportCardCsv(outPath, withHtml, limit)
127→}
128→
129→fun Collection.getCsvMetadataRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
130→    return backend.getCsvMetadataRaw(input)
131→}
132→
133→fun Collection.importCsvRaw(input: ByteArray): ByteArray {
134→    return backend.importCsvRaw(input)
135→}
136→
137→fun Collection.buildSearchString(input: ByteArray): String {
138→    return backend.buildSearchString(SearchNode.parseFrom(input))
139→}
140→

1→import org.gradle.internal.jvm.Jvm
  2→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.tasks.KotlinCompile
  3→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.gradle.tasks.KotlinCompilerExecutionStrategy
  4→
  5→// Top-level build file where you can add configuration options common to all sub-projects/modules.
  6→buildscript {
  7→    // The version for the Kotlin plugin and dependencies
  8→    // If changing this, make sure to update org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization version too
  9→    ext.kotlin_version = '1.9.22'
 10→    ext.lint_version = '31.1.1'
 11→    ext.acra_version = '5.11.3'
 12→    ext.ankidroid_backend_version = '0.1.34-anki23.12.1'
 13→    ext.hamcrest_version = '2.2'
 14→    ext.junit_version = '5.10.2'
 15→    ext.coroutines_version = '1.8.0'
 16→    ext.fragments_version = "1.6.2"
 17→    ext.espresso_version = '3.5.1'
 18→    ext.androidx_test_version = '1.5.0'
 19→    ext.androidx_test_junit_version = '1.1.5'
 20→    ext.robolectric_version = '4.11.1'
 21→    ext.android_gradle_plugin = "8.2.2"
 22→    ext.dokka_version = "1.9.10" // not the same with kotlin version!
 23→    ext.uiautomator_version = "2.3.0-rc01"
 24→
 25→    configurations.configureEach {
 26→        resolutionStrategy.eachDependency { details ->
 27→            if (details.requested.group == 'org.jetbrains.kotlinx'
 28→                    && details.requested.name.contains('kotlinx-serialization-runtime')
 29→                    && details.requested.version.contains('0.11.0')) {
 30→                details.useVersion "0.14.0"
 31→            }
 32→        }
 33→    }
 34→}
 35→
 36→plugins {
 37→    id 'com.android.application' version "$android_gradle_plugin" apply false
 38→    id 'com.android.library' version "$android_gradle_plugin" apply false
 39→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.jvm' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 40→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.android' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 41→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.parcelize' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 42→    id 'org.jetbrains.kotlin.plugin.serialization' version "$kotlin_version" apply false
 43→    id 'org.jlleitschuh.gradle.ktlint' version '11.6.1' apply false
 44→    id 'org.jetbrains.dokka' version "$dokka_version" apply false
 45→}
 46→
 47→Properties localProperties = new Properties()
 48→if (project.rootProject.file('local.properties').exists()) {
 49→    localProperties.load(project.rootProject.file('local.properties').newDataInputStream())
 50→}
 51→Boolean fatalWarnings = !(localProperties['fatal_warnings'] == "false")
 52→
 53→// Here we extract per-module "best practices" settings to a single top-level evaluation
 54→subprojects {
 55→    apply plugin: "org.jlleitschuh.gradle.ktlint"
 56→
 57→    afterEvaluate { project ->
 58→        if (project.hasProperty('android')) {
 59→            project.android.testOptions.unitTests {
 60→                includeAndroidResources = true
 61→            }
 62→            project.android.testOptions.unitTests.all {
 63→                // tell backend to avoid rollover time, and disable interval fuzzing
 64→                environment "ANKI_TEST_MODE", "1"
 65→
 66→                useJUnitPlatform()
 67→                testLogging {
 68→                    events "failed", "skipped"
 69→                    showStackTraces = true
 70→                    exceptionFormat = "full"
 71→                }
 72→
 73→                maxParallelForks = gradleTestMaxParallelForks
 74→                forkEvery = 40
 75→                systemProperties['junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.enabled'] = true
 76→                systemProperties['junit.jupiter.execution.parallel.mode.default'] = "concurrent"
 77→            }
 78→        }
 79→
 80→        /**
 81→         Kotlin allows concrete function implementations inside interfaces.
 82→         For those to work when Kotlin compilation targets the JVM backend, you have to enable the interoperability via
 83→         'freeCompilerArgs' in your gradle file, and you have to choose one of the appropriate '-Xjvm-default' modes.
 84→
 85→         https://kotlinlang.org/docs/java-to-kotlin-interop.html#default-methods-in-interfaces
 86→
 87→         and we used "all" because we don't have downstream consumers
 88→         https://docs.gradle.org/current/userguide/task_configuration_avoidance.html
 89→
 90→         Related to ExperimentalCoroutinesApi: this opt-in is added to enable usage of experimental
 91→            coroutines API, this targets all project modules with the exception of the "api" module,
 92→            which doesn't use coroutines so the annotation isn't not available. This would normally
 93→            result in a warning but we treat warnings as errors.
 94→            (see https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/KT-28777/Using-experimental-coroutines-api-causes-unresolved-dependency)
 95→        */
 96→        tasks.withType(KotlinCompile).configureEach {
 97→            compilerOptions {
 98→                allWarningsAsErrors = fatalWarnings
 99→                def compilerArgs = ['-Xjvm-default=all', '-Xcontext-receivers']
100→                if (project.name != "api") {
101→                    compilerArgs += ['-opt-in=kotlinx.coroutines.ExperimentalCoroutinesApi']
102→                }
103→                freeCompilerArgs = compilerArgs
104→            }
105→        }
106→    }
107→}
108→
109→ext {
110→
111→    jvmVersion = Jvm.current().javaVersion.majorVersion
112→    if (jvmVersion != "17" && jvmVersion != "21") {
113→        println "\n\n\n"
114→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
115→        println "\n\n\n"
116→        println "ERROR: AnkiDroid builds with JVM version 17 or 21."
117→        println "  Incompatible major version detected: '" + jvmVersion + "'"
118→        println "\n\n\n"
119→        println "  If you receive this error because you want to use a newer JDK, we may accept PRs to support new versions."
120→        println "  Edit the main build.gradle file, find this message in the file, and add support for the new version."
121→        println "  Please make sure the `jacocoTestReport` target works on an emulator with our minSdkVersion (currently 23)."
122→        println "\n\n\n"
123→        println "**************************************************************************************************************"
124→        println "\n\n\n"
125→        System.exit(1)
126→    }
127→    ciBuild = System.getenv("CI") == "true" // works for Travis CI or Github Actions
128→    // allows for -Dpre-dex=false to be set
129→    preDexEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("pre-dex", "true")
130→    // allows for universal APKs to be generated
131→    universalApkEnabled = "true" == System.getProperty("universal-apk", "false")
132→
133→    if (System.getProperty("os.name") == "Mac OS X") {
134→        // macOS reports hardware cores. This is accurate for CI, Intel (halved due to SMT) and Apple Silicon
135→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = "sysctl -n hw.physicalcpu".execute().text.toInteger()
136→    } else if (ciBuild) {
137→        // GitHub Actions run on Standard_D4ads_v5 Azure Compute Units with 4 vCPUs
138→        // They appear to be 2:1 vCPU to CPU on Linux/Windows with two vCPU cores but with performance 1:1-similar
139→        // Sources to determine the correct Azure Compute Unit (and get CPU count) to tune this:
140→        // Which Azure compute unit in use? https://github.com/github/docs/blob/a25a33bb6cbf86a629d0a0c7bef624743991f97e/content/actions/using-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners/about-github-hosted-runners.md?plain=1#L176
141→        // What is that compute unit? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/dasv5-dadsv5-series#dadsv5-series
142→        // How does it perform? https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dadsv5 (vs previous Standard_DS2_v2 https://learn.microsoft.com/en-gb/azure/virtual-machines/linux/compute-benchmark-scores#dv2---general-compute)
143→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = 4
144→    } else {
145→        // Use 50% of cores to account for SMT which doesn't help this workload
146→        gradleTestMaxParallelForks = Runtime.runtime.availableProcessors().intdiv(2) ?: 1
147→    }
148→}
149→
